nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## Retrieve the Background Image and fetch Meta from the File
### Like Sizes, to properly place the "Top Image" a.k.a "Watermark" a.k.a "Overlay" above the "Background"-Image

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## Calculate the Position for the "Top" Image













We want to place the "Top"-Image it dead-center on the "Background"-Image. But the upper-left-corner is the origin for the operation. 

You may adjust it to your needs, to – for example adjust the size of your Overlay-Image, or place it in some corner. Adjust the Formular to your needs.

**⚠️ Limitation:** The Image that Overlays the Background-Image has to be <= the size of the background image to work properly.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content: ### 🖼️ Binary Property *bg* should now be the composite image and be overlayed by *top*

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Note3

parameters.content:
## Instructions

This automation *overlays* a `background` image with another image, making it easy to add watermarks or logos.

You can use this automation to **watermark** your images by overlaying them with a transparent version of your logo. If you'd like to **place your logo in a specific corner**, feel free to _adjust the position_ of the overlay image in the code node.

### How it Works

1. Both images are downloaded, so we can process binary files (you can modify the source, tho.)
2. We extract metadata, focusing on the dimensions of each image.
3. The position of the overlay image is calculated (default: dead center of the background image).
4. The two images are *composited* together.

### Limitations and Optimization Opportunities

1. The overlay image must be the same size or smaller than the background image for proper alignment.
2. The overlay image does not automatically scale to match the proportions of the background image.

![Image]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}  
Enjoy the workflow! ❤️  
[let the workf low]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} — Workflow Automation & Development

parameters.content:
## Instructions

This automation *overlays* a `background` image with another image, making it easy to add watermarks or logos.

You can use this automation to **watermark** your images by overlaying them with a transparent version of your logo. If you'd like to **place your logo in a specific corner**, feel free to _adjust the position_ of the overlay image in the code node.

### How it Works

1. Both images are downloaded, so we can process binary files (you can modify the source, tho.)
2. We extract metadata, focusing on the dimensions of each image.
3. The position of the overlay image is calculated (default: dead center of the background image).
4. The two images are *composited* together.

### Limitations and Optimization Opportunities

1. The overlay image must be the same size or smaller than the background image for proper alignment.
2. The overlay image does not automatically scale to match the proportions of the background image.

![Image]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}  
Enjoy the workflow! ❤️

## Retrieve the Background Image and fetch Meta from the File
### Like Sizes, to properly place the "Top Image" a.k.a "Watermark" a.k.a "Overlay" above the "Background"-Image

## Calculate the Position for the "Top" Image













We want to place the "Top"-Image it dead-center on the "Background"-Image. But the upper-left-corner is the origin for the operation. 

You may adjust it to your needs, to – for example adjust the size of your Overlay-Image, or place it in some corner. Adjust the Formular to your needs.

**⚠️ Limitation:** The Image that Overlays the Background-Image has to be <= the size of the background image to work properly.
### 🖼️ Binary Property *bg* should now be the composite image and be overlayed by *top*

nodes.name: Wait for both Images and merge Binary in one Item

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: No custom credentials required for utility-only operations.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking "Test workflow"".
Open the "Get Meta BG" node (n8n-nodes-base.editImage) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Logo for the Watermark" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get the Image for Background" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait for both Images and merge Binary in one Item" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get Meta Top" node (n8n-nodes-base.editImage) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Let "top" overlay "bg"" node (n8n-nodes-base.editImage) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Nest Top Meta".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Nest Bg Meta".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
